description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a rectangular footprint and five visible floors. The facade is clad in light gray brick or concrete, with regularly spaced windows and prominent balconies on two sides, each balcony featuring green foliage or planter boxes. The front of the building has a central entrance door and symmetrical window arrangements, while the sides and rear have fewer windows and less ornamentation. The roof is flat, with some open areas and partial walls suggesting rooftop access or communal spaces. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function. The building appears to be constructed primarily from concrete and glass, with metal railings and some wooden window frames.
description: A five-story, light gray apartment building with symmetrical windows and green balconies on two sides, featuring a flat roof and a central entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, symmetrical window arrangement, and stacked balconies with greenery on both sides.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the row of balconies and a large window, with minimal detailing on the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view with fewer windows, no balconies, and a mostly plain facade.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing another row of balconies and a single window at the base.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns on the front facade, with regular spacing and alignment. Side and rear facades have fewer, more irregularly spaced windows.
